items.title: Explosives incident outside Zohran Mamdani's home was ISIS-inspired, U.S. attorney says
items.standfirst: Officials took questions Monday after federal terrorism charges were filed against two men in an explosives incident outside New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's home. Following the remarks from the authorities, CBS News' Anna Schecter provided more details.
items.content: Officials took questions Monday after federal terrorism charges were filed against two men in an explosives incident outside New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani's home. Following the remarks from the authorities, CBS News' Anna Schecter provided more details.

items.title: Charges announced, suspects in court after explosives thrown outside Zohran Mamdani's home

items.title: Attempted attack with explosives in New York City investigated as "ISIS-inspired terrorism"
items.standfirst: Two men have been charged with allegedly providing support to a foreign terrorism organization and using a weapon of mass destruction. New York City NYPD Commissioner says the explosive devices "could have caused serious injury or death."
items.content: Two men have been charged with allegedly providing support to a foreign terrorism organization and using a weapon of mass destruction. New York City NYPD Commissioner says the explosive devices "could have caused serious injury or death."(Image credit: Joseph B. Frederick)

clusters.title: Istanbul's mayor on trial for corruption charges
clusters.neutral_headline: Istanbul Mayor On Trial For Corruption

items.title: Istanbul's mayor on trial for corruption charges
items.standfirst: Ekrem Imamoglu is seen as Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival.
items.content: Ekrem Imamoglu is seen as Turkish President Recep Tayyip Erdogan's main political rival.

items.title: Jailed Istanbul mayor goes on trial in corruption case slammed as political
items.standfirst: Istanbul's former mayor Ekrem Imamoglu has gone on trial in Turkey on charges of corruption, in a landmark case widely criticised by rights groups. Imamoglu was arrested almost one year ago, when the opposition CHP party named him as their presidential candidate. Critics say the trial is an attempt to scupper Imamoglu's chances of beating Recep Tayyip Erdogan at the ballot box.
items.content: Istanbul's former mayor Ekrem Imamoglu has gone on trial in Turkey on charges of corruption, in a landmark case widely criticised by rights groups. Imamoglu was arrested almost one year ago, when the opposition CHP party named him as their presidential candidate. Critics say the trial is an attempt to scupper Imamoglu's chances of beating Recep Tayyip Erdogan at the ballot box.

items.title: Landmark trial begins for Turkish opposition leader Imamoglu
items.standfirst: Istanbul's jailed mayor Ekrem Imamoglu went on trial on Monday in a widespread corruption case which critics say is a politically motivated bid to eliminate his chances of challenging President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.
items.content: Istanbul's jailed mayor Ekrem Imamoglu went on trial on Monday in a widespread corruption case which critics say is a politically motivated bid to eliminate his chances of challenging President Recep Tayyip Erdogan.

items.title: Turkey’s top opposition politician stands trial on corruption charges

items.content: Istanbul’s deposed mayor Ekrem İmamoğlu accused of running a criminal organisation

items.title: Frost pregnancy tip came from 'good source', ex-Mail journalist tells court
items.standfirst: Katie Nicholl denies she got sensitive medical information about the actress though unlawful means.
items.content: Katie Nicholl denies she got sensitive medical information about the actress though unlawful means.

items.title: Ex-Mail on Sunday journalist denies ordering ‘blag’ of Sadie Frost’s medical information
items.standfirst: Katie Nicholl’s name appears on many of the stories that Frost, Prince Harry and others have complained aboutA senior former Mail on Sunday journalist has denied commissioning a “blag” of sensitive medical information about Sadie Frost that the actor had not even told her own mother.At the high court, Katie Nicholl, a former diary editor and royal editor at the paper, was accused of using blagged information from a private investigator to uncover “extraordinarily intrusive” details of Frost’s medical history. Continue reading...

clusters.title: Markets plunge as price of oil reaches 4-year high
clusters.neutral_headline: Oil Price Reaches Four Year High

items.title: Markets plunge as price of oil reaches 4-year high
items.standfirst: Stock markets plunged on Monday as the price of oil hit more than $100 per barrel for the first time since July 2022. Noam Raydan, a senior fellow at the Washington Institute for Near East Policy, joins with more.
items.content: Stock markets plunged on Monday as the price of oil hit more than $100 per barrel for the first time since July 2022. Noam Raydan, a senior fellow at the Washington Institute for Near East Policy, joins with more.

items.title: Oil hits $100 per barrel for first time since July 2022
items.standfirst: The price of oil surged past $100 on Sunday for the first time since July 2022 as the ripple effects from the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran continued to hit global markets.
items.content: The price of oil surged past $100 on Sunday for the first time since July 2022 as the ripple effects from the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran continued to hit global markets.

ai_summary: Oil price surpasses $100 per barrel
Stock markets plunge in response

clusters.title: Ex-rapper Balendra Shah set to be Nepal PM after party’s landslide election win
clusters.neutral_headline: Rapper Turned Politician Wins Nepal Election

items.title: Ex-rapper Balendra Shah set to be Nepal PM after party’s landslide election win
items.standfirst: Shah’s Rastriya Swatantra party secures thumping victory in first poll since gen Z protests that toppled government Balendra Shah, the rapper turned politician and popular figurehead of a gen Z revolution, looks set to become Nepal’s next prime minister after his party won by an unprecedented margin.Shah, known widely as Balen, and his Rastriya Swatantra party (RSP) secured a rare landslide victory in the first election since youth-led protests during which dozens were killed and the former government was toppled. Continue reading...
items.content: Shah’s Rastriya Swatantra party secures thumping victory in first poll since gen Z protests that toppled government Balendra Shah, the rapper turned politician and popular figurehead of a gen Z revolution, looks set to become Nepal’s next prime minister after his party won by an unprecedented margin.Shah, known widely as Balen, and his Rastriya Swatantra party (RSP) secured a rare landslide victory in the first election since youth-led protests during which dozens were killed and the former government was toppled. Continue reading...

items.title: Rapper-politician Balendra Shah unseats Nepal's ex-PM as he heads for victory
items.standfirst: Thursday's general election was the first since violent youth-led protests toppled the government in September.
items.content: Thursday's general election was the first since violent youth-led protests toppled the government in September.

ai_summary: Balendra Shah's party wins landslide victory
Shah set to become Nepal's next prime minister

Shah's party secures rare landslide victory
